video_summary: This video introduces RESTful API architecture and its best practices, distinguishing between client-side and server-side rendering while explaining common HTTP methods (GET, POST, DELETE) for data operations, emphasizing SSR for the tutorial series.

concepts.colloquial_context: rest app, restful app is different it's a state it requires to have particular rules these can be called best practices
concepts.description: An architectural style for designing networked applications, characterized by being stateless, client-server, cacheable, layered, and having a uniform interface, often following specific best practices.

concepts.colloquial_context: get tu slash user, user data ko read kar ke read karo
concepts.description: An HTTP method used to request data from a specified resource. GET requests should only retrieve data and have no other effect on the data.
An HTTP method used to send data to a server to create or update a resource. It often results in a change in state or side effects on the server.

concepts.colloquial_context: tilit karna hai, dilit diaga liye thi ravt karni hai
concepts.description: An HTTP method used to request that a specified resource be deleted.

concepts.description: Specific URLs that an API exposes, allowing clients to interact with particular resources or functionalities on the server.

concepts.colloquial_context: client side rendering weiber side rendering it slow
concepts.description: A web rendering approach where the browser downloads a minimal HTML page and JavaScript, which then renders the full content dynamically in the user's browser.

concepts.colloquial_context: server side rendering is very fast as we get the data we have to follow this SSR thing in our tutorial series
concepts.description: A web rendering approach where the server processes the request and sends a fully rendered HTML page to the client, which the browser can then display immediately.

concepts.colloquial_context: best practices, pehla rule, agar buticol rule kya kehta hai
concepts.description: Guidelines and conventions for designing robust, scalable, and maintainable APIs, often related to consistent use of HTTP methods, status codes, and URL structures.

concepts.colloquial_context: User data ko read kar ke read karo
concepts.description: The process of fetching existing data from a server or database, typically performed using the HTTP GET method.

concepts.colloquial_context: Yojar ko yojar ko abdet kar lega, post route for update
concepts.description: The process of modifying existing data on a server or database, often performed using HTTP POST or PUT methods.
